title: Fix Excel re-import to update records by item ID, rename import/export buttons

requirements.text: When importing an Excel file in the Inventory page, use the item ID (ID Barang) as the stable key: if a row's ID matches an existing item, update that item's other fields (name, price, stock, etc.) with the values from Excel instead of skipping or duplicating it. Only create a new item if the ID does not match any existing record.

quotes: saat mengimpor kembali ke aplikasi, data yang di edit di exle tidak berubah di aplikasi, mungkin bisa ID barang sebagai ID yg baku dan data yang lainya bisa berubah sesuai perubahan di excel

acceptanceCriteria: Downloading the inventory as Excel, editing a row's name or price in Excel, then re-uploading the file causes the corresponding item in the app to reflect the edited values.
An item whose ID exists in both the app and the Excel file is updated, not duplicated.
A row in Excel with an ID not found in the app is added as a new item.
Items in the app that have no matching row in the uploaded Excel file remain unchanged.

requirements.text: On the Inventory page, rename the 'Impor Excel' button label to 'Unggah' and the 'Ekspor Excel' button label to 'Unduh'.

quotes: untuk tombol Impor excel dan expor excel diganti unggah dan unduh saja

acceptanceCriteria: The button previously labeled 'Impor Excel' now displays 'Unggah'.
The button previously labeled 'Ekspor Excel' now displays 'Unduh'.
Both buttons retain their existing functionality.

constraints: Item ID must be treated as the immutable primary key during Excel import matching.
Do not change the backend data schema or item ID generation logic.
nonGoals: Changing any other page besides the Inventory page.
Modifying the Excel file format or column structure.
Adding new columns or fields to the exported Excel file.
